channel.title: NYT > World News
channel.copyright: Copyright 2026 The New York Times Company

item.title: Pentagon Orders 2,000 Airborne Troops to Middle East
item.description: The deployment could give President Trump more military options after a 15-point proposal, delivered via Pakistan, was sent to the Iranians, according to two officials briefed on the diplomacy.
item.dc:creator: The New York Times
item.media:credit: Avishag Shaar-Yashuv for The New York Times

item.title: The U.S. Military Said It Helped Bomb a Drug Camp in Ecuador. It Was a Dairy Farm.
item.description: The Times visited a village where the United States and Ecuador said they destroyed an armed group’s training camp. Residents said it was actually a dairy farm.
item.dc:creator: Luis Ferré-Sadurní, José María León Cabrera, Annie Correal, Eric Schmitt and Federico Rios
item.category: United States Defense and Military Forces
item.media:description: The remains of a dairy cattle farm in San Martín that was burned and bombed by the Ecuadorean military.

item.title: How Pakistan Is Trying to Reshape Its Image Abroad
item.description: New, friendly media operations and expanded state-run television are pushing Pakistan’s message while independent news outlets face repression.
item.dc:creator: Elian Peltier and Zia ur-Rehman
item.media:credit: Asim Hafeez for The New York Times
item.media:description: A live news broadcast at Asia One, a new 24/7 news channel, in Karachi earlier this year.

item.title: Investigators Seek Answers in Attacks on Jewish Sites in Europe
item.description: Attacks on schools and property in several countries have Jewish communities on edge, amid suspicions that Iran is behind the violence.
item.dc:creator: Adam Goldman and Lizzie Dearden
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)
item.media:description: Belgian military personnel guarding a synagogue in Antwerp on Monday.

item.title: Costa Rica Agrees to Take Migrants Deported by the Trump Administration
item.description: The agreement is part of President Trump’s efforts to find governments willing to accept people who have been detained in the United States.
item.dc:creator: Emiliano Rodríguez Mega
item.media:description: Then-Homeland Security Secretary Kristi Noem with President Rodrigo Chaves Robles of Costa Rica during a news conference last year in San José, Costa Rica.

item.title: Italy’s Meloni Seemed Unbeatable. A Referendum Defeat Has Dented Her Aura.
item.description: Prime Minister Giorgia Meloni had led one of Italy’s most stable postwar governments. Now she’s under pressure after failing to convince Italians to back a judicial overhaul.
item.dc:creator: Motoko Rich
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)
item.media:description: Prime Minister Giorgia Meloni in Paris in January. Voters on Monday rejected her plan to overhaul Italy’s judiciary.

item.title: Government Cuts Gut the Memory of Argentina’s Dirty War
item.description: Fifty years after the military dictatorship, Argentina’s government is defunding human rights groups and promoting a revisionist account of the junta’s crimes.
item.dc:creator: Emma Bubola and Sarah Pabst
item.category: Human Rights and Human Rights Violations
War Crimes, Genocide and Crimes Against Humanity
Coups D'Etat and Attempted Coups D'Etat
Grandmothers of the Plaza de Mayo (Argentina)
Mothers of the Plaza de Mayo
item.media:credit: Sarah Pabst for The New York Times
item.media:description: The mothers of the disappeared marching in Buenos Aires’ Plaza de Mayo this month.

item.title: Trump Sours on Keir Starmer Over UK’s Decision Not to Join Attacks on Iran
item.description: President Trump once called Prime Minister Keir Starmer a friend. But Britain’s decision not to join the attacks on Iran has led to merciless mocking by the president.
item.dc:creator: Michael D. Shear
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)
item.media:description: President Donald Trump and Prime Minister Keir Starmer at Chequers, the official estate of Britain’s leader, in September 2025.

item.title: Afghanistan Frees American Dennis Coyle from Detention
item.description: Dennis Walter Coyle, a researcher from Colorado, had been held since last year by the Taliban government.
item.dc:creator: Elian Peltier
item.media:credit: Kiana Hayeri for The New York Times
item.media:description: The American researcher Dennis Walter Coyle, center, in Kabul, Afghanistan, on Tuesday.

item.title: Ukraine Spent Big to Shield Energy Industry From Drones. Is the Mideast Next?
item.description: With the use of electronic jamming systems and interceptor drones, the Ukrainian national oil and gas company may be a model for others.
item.dc:creator: Andrew E. Kramer
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)
item.media:description: A damaged area of a Naftogaz gas facility following Russian missile and drone attacks, in an undisclosed location in Ukraine in December.

item.title: As Denmark Goes to the Polls, Here’s What to Know About the Election and Key Issues
item.description: President Trump’s threats to take Greenland away from Denmark have lifted Prime Minister Mette Frederiksen, who appeared the front-runner as polls opened.
item.dc:creator: Jeffrey Gettleman and Maya Tekeli
item.media:credit: Mikkel Berg Pedersen/Ritzau Scanpix, via Agence France-Presse — Getty Images
item.media:description: Preparing a polling station in Aarhus, Denmark, on Tuesday.

item.title: How North Korea’s Kim Jong-un Is Using the Iran War to Justify His Nuclear Arsenal
item.description: He delivered a lengthy speech at his country’s rubber-stamp Parliament, declaring that his nuclear power will shield his country from American hostility.
item.dc:creator: Choe Sang-Hun
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)
item.media:credit: Korean Central News Agency/Korea News Service, via Associated Press
item.media:description: A photo released by North Korean state media showing the country’s leader, Kim Jong-un, speaking at Parliament in Pyongyang on Monday.

item.title: Take Shorter Showers: South Korea Kicks Off an Energy-Saving Drive
item.description: President Lee Jae Myung called on the public to cooperate, likening the energy supply disruption caused by the Iran war to the Asian financial crisis and the pandemic.
item.dc:creator: Choe Sang-Hun
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)
item.media:description: South Korea’s government is encouraging people to use public transportation to help conserve energy.

item.title: A Missile Fragment in a Schoolyard
item.description: Examining an image of children near a missile fragment, which landed near a school as several families were sheltering inside it.
item.dc:creator: Avishag Shaar-Yashuv
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)
item.media:description: In One Image A Missile Fragment in a Schoolyard by Avishag Shaar-Yashuv

item.title: Sarah Mullally, the First Female Archbishop of Canterbury, is About to Be Enthroned
item.description: Sarah Mullally will be installed on Wednesday at Canterbury Cathedral. Her appointment to the role has been both celebrated, and denounced by some factions within the global Anglican Church.
item.dc:creator: Megan Specia
item.media:description: The ceremony in Canterbury on Wednesday is the symbolic start of Sarah Mullally’s ministry, after she was legally confirmed as archbishop at St. Paul’s Cathedral in London in January.

item.title: Gambia Says the Island Is Cursed. Migrants Saw an Opportunity.
item.description: Thousands of African migrants hoping to reach Europe have flocked to a remote island in Gambia that local villagers say is protected by a curse.
item.dc:creator: Saikou Jammeh and Muhamadou Bittaye
item.media:description: A damaged wooden pirogue used by migrants rests along the shore of Jinack Island, in Gambia, on Sunday.

item.title: Plan to Disarm Hamas in Gaza Is Detailed at U.N.
item.description: A Board of Peace member said the most dangerous weapons would be collected first. He linked compliance with disarmament to reconstruction beginning in the enclave.
item.dc:creator: Ephrat Livni
item.media:credit: Saher Alghorra for The New York Times
item.media:description: Hamas fighters in Gaza City in November.

item.title: Iran Says ‘Non-Hostile’ Ships Can Sail Through the Strait of Hormuz
item.description: Ships with no ties to Israel or the United States would be allowed to pass, the government said, but it was unclear if any vessels would try.
item.dc:creator: Peter Eavis
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)
item.media:description: Cargo ships in the Gulf, near the Strait of Hormuz, seen from the United Arab Emirates earlier this month.

item.title: Here’s What Happened in the War in the Middle East on Tuesday
item.description: The U.S. was said to have sent Iran a peace plan via Pakistan on a day that the Iranians fired a torrent of missiles across the region.
item.dc:creator: Anushka Patil
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)
item.media:description: Emergency workers inspecting the site of a strike in a residential area of the northwestern city of Tabriz, Iran, on Tuesday.

item.title: After Standing Up to Trump Over Greenland, Denmark’s Prime Minister Leads in Election
item.description: Mette Frederiksen’s party fell far short of a majority, but analysts say she is still in the best position to form a new government.
item.dc:creator: Maya Tekeli and Jeffrey Gettleman
item.media:credit: Hilary Swift for The New York Times
item.media:description: Denmark’s prime minister, Mette Frederiksen. Her party came in first in Tuesday’s election, but won less than a quarter of the vote.

item.title: Iran Signals Resilience With Volley of Missiles Across Middle East
item.description: The barrage continued as officials said the U.S. had sent Iran a 15-point plan to end the war. But Iranian officials have publicly denied that Washington and Tehran are talking directly.
item.dc:creator: Yeganeh Torbati, Gabby Sobelman, Erica L. Green and Thomas Fuller
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)
United States Defense and Military Forces
item.media:credit: Avishag Shaar-Yashuv for The New York Times
item.media:description: Gathering around a fragment of a missile that landed in an orchard near the Palestinian village of Hares in the West Bank on Tuesday.

item.title: Asia’s Energy Crisis Stings
item.description: The energy crisis unleashed by the war in Iran may already be worse than the oil shocks of the 1970s. Here’s how it’s upending lives.
item.dc:creator: Katrin Bennhold
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)
Far East, South and Southeast Asia and Pacific Areas

item.title: Russia Launches Daytime Attack on Ukraine With Over 500 Drones
item.description: The assault, which came after overnight strikes across the country, was one of the largest of the war, the Ukrainian authorities said.
item.dc:creator: Cassandra Vinograd

item.title: Pakistan Offers to Host Talks Between Iran and the United States
item.description: Pakistan, which has cultivated ties with both Washington and Tehran, sees a diplomatic opening to intervene in the war in Iran, its neighbor.
item.dc:creator: Elian Peltier
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)
item.media:description: Prime Minister Shehbaz Sharif of Pakistan, right, with President Trump during last year’s Gaza peace summit in Egypt.

item.title: United States Said to Have Sent Iran a Plan to End the Middle East War
item.description: The 15-point plan was delivered via Pakistan, whose army chief has emerged as the key interlocutor between the United States and Iran, officials say.
item.dc:creator: Adam Rasgon, Julian E. Barnes and Farnaz Fassihi
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)
item.media:credit: Arash Khamooshi for The New York Times
item.media:description: An apartment shattered by an airstrike in Tehran on Monday.

item.title: High Oil and Gas Prices Could Outlast Trump’s War With Iran
item.description: While the president has promised rapid relief, Americans could feel the financial sting of the conflict for some time after it ends.
item.dc:creator: Tony Romm and Rebecca F. Elliott
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)
item.media:credit: Tierney L. Cross/The New York Times
item.media:description: Even when the conflict ends, it may be weeks or months before American families and businesses see a true break in spiraling energy costs.

item.title: Lebanon Expels Iranian Envoy as Rift With Tehran Deepens
item.description: The decision by Lebanon’s foreign ministry has heightened fears of internal instability. Hezbollah, the Iranian-backed militant group and political party, was quick to condemn the move.
item.dc:creator: Euan Ward
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)
item.media:description: Mohammad Reza Shibani, Iran’s ambassador to Beirut, has been ordered to leave Lebanon by the end of the week.

item.title: Qatar Is Not Mediating U.S.-Iran Talks, Foreign Minister Says
item.description: Qatar’s Foreign Ministry warned that the regional security system has been shattered, as the war has destroyed trust between Gulf countries and Iran.
item.dc:creator: Ismaeel Naar
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)
item.media:description: Majed al-Ansari, Qatar’s Foreign Ministry spokesman, said the country isn’t involved with mediation talks.

item.title: Reopening the Strait of Hormuz will be difficult without a deal with Iran.
item.dc:creator: Agnes Chang, Adina Renner and Samuel Granados

item.title: Why President Trump Has a Big Oil Problem
item.description: Our national security correspondent David E. Sanger looks at President Trump’s trouble handling retaliatory attacks by Iran that have largely choked off the Strait of Hormuz.
item.dc:creator: David E. Sanger, Coleman Lowndes, Laura Salaberry, Nikolay Nikolov, Rafaela Balster, Alexandra Ostasiewicz and Whitney Shefte
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)

item.title: Russian Oil Shipment Puts Focus on Kremlin Spy Outpost in Cuba
item.description: Moscow may be challenging President Trump’s effort to choke Cuba’s economy. China also has suspected listening posts on the island.
item.dc:creator: Michael Crowley
item.category: United States Defense and Military Forces
item.media:description: Mr. Trump has spoken of using his economic leverage to mount a “friendly takeover” of Cuba and is pressing for the ouster of its president, Miguel Díaz-Canel.

item.title: Iran’s New Security Chief Is a Hard-Line Former Guards Commander
item.description: Mohammad Bagher Zolghadr replaces Ali Larijani, who was killed last week in an Israeli strike. He has a history of expanding the Guards’ reach into Iran’s politics.
item.dc:creator: Yeganeh Torbati
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)
item.media:description: Mohammad Bagher Zolghadr in 2024.

item.title: Israel Plans to Control Large Parts of Southern Lebanon, Defense Minister Says
item.description: Israel Katz, the Israeli defense minister, suggested that Israeli troops might remain in parts of Lebanon even after the fighting there winds down.
item.dc:creator: Natan Odenheimer
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)
item.media:description: Israeli military vehicles near the border of Lebanon on Saturday.

item.title: Mel Schilling, Dating Expert on ‘Married at First Sight,’ Dies at 54
item.description: Ms. Schilling, an Australian psychologist, was diagnosed with colon cancer in 2023. Two weeks ago she said the disease had spread to her brain.
item.dc:creator: Claire Moses
item.media:description: Mel Schilling at the 2025 BAFTA Awards.

item.title: Three of the Lives Lost in One Day of Israeli Strikes in Lebanon
item.description: A 4-year-old, a professor and an aid worker are among those killed in separate airstrikes as Israel pummels towns and cities, saying it is targeting Hezbollah operatives.
item.dc:creator: Euan Ward
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)
item.media:credit: Diego Ibarra Sanchez for The New York Times
item.media:description: The aftermath of a strike in Beirut, Lebanon, on Wednesday.

item.title: Philippines Declares National Emergency Over High Fuel Prices
item.description: The Southeast Asian country imports 90 percent of its oil from the Middle East, and the government is under intense pressure to deal with the situation.
item.dc:creator: Sui-Lee Wee
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)
item.media:description: Lining up at a gas station in the Manila area this month. Transportation workers in the Philippines have called for mass protests this week over fuel prices.

item.title: Europe Clinches Critical Mineral Access With Australian Trade Deal
item.description: The European Union and Australia have shaken hands on a trade deal that would improve access to aluminum, lithium and other critical minerals for the 27-nation bloc.
item.dc:creator: Jeanna Smialek and Victoria Kim
item.media:description: Ursula von der Leyen, the president of the European Union’s executive branch, and Prime Minister Anthony Albanese of Australia at a signing ceremony in Canberra on Tuesday.

item.title: Tango Therapy: How the Dance of Passion Is Helping Parkinson’s Patients
item.description: Once a week, patients in an Argentine hospital with Parkinson’s disease use the movements of tango to help address issues of balance, stiffness and coordination.
item.dc:creator: Magalí Druscovich and Pam Belluck

item.title: Here’s the latest.
item.dc:creator: Eric Schmitt, Yeganeh Torbati, Gabby Sobelman and Erica L. Green

item.title: Hasan Piker Defends Upscale Lodging During Humanitarian Mission in Cuba
item.description: He helped bring attention to a group delivering humanitarian aid to Cuba, which was criticized for staying in an upscale hotel as Cubans faced another blackout.
item.dc:creator: Annie Correal
item.media:credit: Adali Schell for The New York Times
item.media:description: Hasan Piker in his studio in California last year.

item.title: Trump Delays Threat to Iran, but War Negotiations Are in Early Stage
item.description: President Trump postponed his threat to strike power plants in Iran, citing “productive conversations” with the Iranians. But officials said the talks were in an early stage and not substantive.
item.dc:creator: Tyler Pager, David E. Sanger and Farnaz Fassihi
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)
item.media:credit: Arash Khamooshi for The New York Times
item.media:description: Abbas Araghchi, Iran’s foreign minister, in Tehran last year. He is said to have conveyed to the U.S. that Iran would need sanctions relief and guarantees against future attacks.

item.title: Here’s What Happened in the War in the Middle East on Monday
item.description: Conflicting signals about whether any negotiations to end the fighting were in progress created confusion.
item.dc:creator: Ephrat Livni
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)
item.media:credit: Diego Ibarra Sanchez for The New York Times
item.media:description: The outskirts of Beirut, Lebanon, Monday night. Airstrikes hit the southern parts of the capital known as a Hezbollah stronghold soon after Israel issued evacuation warnings.

item.title: Trump Says U.S. Is Negotiating With Iran, Which Publicly Denies His Claim
item.description: “Very, very strong talks,” President Trump claimed. Fake news, replied an Iranian parliamentary leader.
item.dc:creator: Luke Broadwater, Aaron Boxerman, Erika Solomon and Thomas Fuller
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)
item.media:credit: Arash Khamooshi for The New York Times
item.media:description: An apartment building hit by a strike in Tehran on Monday.

item.title: ‘Strong Talks’ With Iran?
item.description: President Trump’s latest statements about discussions with Iran don’t necessarily signal an end in sight to the war, but they do buy him some time.
item.dc:creator: Katrin Bennhold
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)

item.title: Trump Delays Energy Strikes, but Iran’s Infrastructure Is Already Battered
item.description: U.S. and Israeli attacks on power plants and other civilian infrastructure risk escalating the conflict across the region, and angering Iranians who oppose the government.
item.dc:creator: Erika Solomon
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)
item.media:credit: Arash Khamooshi for The New York Times
item.media:description: The aftermath of a strike on an oil storage facility in Tehran earlier this month.

item.title: Energy Crisis Will Not Be Resolved Quickly if War Ends, I.E.A. Chief Warns
item.description: Fatih Birol, the leader of the International Energy Agency, said the Iran war was a bigger crisis than the two oil shocks in the 1970s combined.
item.dc:creator: Ravi Mattu
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)
item.media:credit: Lukas Coch/Australian Associated Press, via Reuters
item.media:description: Fatih Birol, the executive director of the International Energy Agency, speaks in Canberra, Australia, on Monday.

item.title: Plane Crash Kills 66 From Military and Police in Colombia
item.description: A military aircraft transporting 128 troops and crew members was in an accident as it took off from southern Colombia, military officials said.
item.dc:creator: Luis Ferré-Sadurní and Federico Rios
item.media:description: The aircraft had taken off from Puerto Leguízamo, Colombia, at 9:50 a.m. There were 114 soldiers and police officers and 11 crew members on board, officials said.

item.title: Israel Says Its Own Artillery Killed a Farmer, Not a Hezbollah Rocket
item.description: Ofer Moskovitz, 60, an avocado farmer in a kibbutz on Israel’s border with Lebanon, was killed Sunday when the car he was riding in was hit.
item.dc:creator: David M. Halbfinger and Johnatan Reiss
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)
item.media:description: Ofer Moskovitz, 60, holding avocados on a dirt road near Israel’s border with Lebanon last week.

item.title: Iran attempted to attack a far-off military base.
item.dc:creator: Martín González Gómez

item.title: Italians Reject Judicial Overhaul, Undermining Meloni
item.description: Some 54 percent of voters spurned a government plan to change how jurists are supervised. It presented Prime Minister Giorgia Meloni with her biggest domestic challenge since her election in 2022.
item.dc:creator: Motoko Rich
item.media:description: Prime Minister Giorgia Meloni in Rome last month. She has staked considerable political capital on campaigning for the measures.

item.title: Lionel Jospin, Who Helped Set France on Socialist Path, Dies at 88
item.description: As prime minister beginning in 1997, he introduced a national 35-hour workweek and oversaw the replacement of the franc with the euro.
item.dc:creator: David A. Andelman
item.media:description: Lionel Jospin delivering a speech in Paris in 2002. Through his parents, who were socialists, Mr. Jospin said he learned “the consequences of social misery, and the desire to change all of that.”

item.title: Why the BTS Comeback Concert Was a ‘Disaster’ for Some Businesses
item.description: The turnout for the K-pop titans’ show was much lower than projected by officials, hitting the bottom line of some restaurants. Shares in the group’s management company also fell.
item.dc:creator: Jin Yu Young and Yan Zhuang
item.media:credit: Jun Michael Park for The New York Times
item.media:description: City officials were expecting more than a quarter of a million people at Gwanghwamun Square in central Seoul on Saturday.

item.title: Sidelined by War With Iran, Gaza Residents Remain in Limbo
item.description: The new war has led to panic buying and a surge in food prices for Gazans as they try to recover from Israel’s two-year offensive against Hamas.
item.dc:creator: Bilal Shbair, Isabel Kershner and Abu Bakr Bashir
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)
item.media:credit: Saher Alghorra for The New York Times

item.title: A Choice of Deadly Drones Is Only a Few Clicks Away for Ukrainian Troops
item.description: Ukraine has created online marketplaces to let units select their own drones, a break from generations of standardized and centralized weapons procurement.
item.dc:creator: Maria Varenikova
item.media:description: A Ukrainian soldier arming a drone in the Kharkiv region last year.

item.title: Energy Attacks in War on Iran Could Turn Economic Shock Into Long-Term Damage
item.description: A new phase targeting oil and gas infrastructure in the Persian Gulf threatens to hurt businesses and customers around the world for months or even years.
item.dc:creator: Patricia Cohen
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)
item.media:credit: Lauren DeCicca for The New York Times
item.media:description: Hand-painted signs at a gas station in Samut Prakan, Thailand, last week said the station was out of diesel and 95-octane gasoline.

item.title: Canada May Need to Lean on the US as Military Threats in the Arctic Rise
item.description: For the past seven decades, Canada has been the junior partner in a military agreement with the United States to protect the Canadian Arctic.
item.dc:creator: Norimitsu Onishi
item.category: United States Defense and Military Forces
item.media:credit: Renaud Philippe for The New York Times
item.media:description: A North Warning System station near Cambridge Bay on Victoria Island in Canada’s Arctic. The system consists of automated radars whose technology is now widely considered outdated in the era of satellites.

item.title: Canada’s Military Wants to Prove It Can Defend the High Arctic
item.description: Canadian soldiers transported M777 howitzers to the High Arctic to show their ability to fight in an increasingly contested part of the world. It did not go as planned.
item.dc:creator: Norimitsu Onishi and Renaud Philippe

item.title: Diabetes, Overlooked and Unchecked, Poses New Risks in Africa
item.description: As deaths from diabetes start to rival those from infectious threats like malaria, a new form of the condition linked to malnutrition is surfacing in patients who can afford neither screening nor care.
item.dc:creator: Stephanie Nolen and Arlette Bashizi
item.media:credit: Arlette Bashizi for The New York Times
item.media:description: Habiba Sali, right, a nurse in the endocrinology department of the Maroua Regional Hospital in the far north region of Cameroon, took the vital signs of patient Aminatou Mana during a regular checkup.
